proposals.title: UP_TRI × Bear: n=96, 94.7% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, regime=Bear (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +31.7pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.8815 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.61 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)

Portfolio currently shows Choppy regime concentration (100%) and DOWN_TRI dominance (19/24); when Bear regime returns, this Bear-gated UP_TRI boost will activate against an already-stretched book — monitor at regime rotation
R-multiple 0.61 reflects Day-6 forced-exit dominance per D-8 review; if exit policy changes, cohort statistics may need re-baselining
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=96, wr=94.7%, edge +31.7pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=96, Wilson lower 0.8815, +31.7pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.61. Cohort is regime-gated to Bear while current regime is Choppy (stable, 7 days) with zero Bear exposure, so activation is deferred and existing UP_TRI/Choppy concentration is not immediately compounded. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-04) and no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: UP_TRI × Metal: n=17, 88.2% WR, Tier M → boost candidate (TAKE_SMALL)

claim.what: Promote boost_pattern: signal=UP_TRI, sector=Metal (Tier B / TAKE_SMALL)
claim.expected_effect: Surface as Tier-B boost on cohort matches; observed +25.3pp edge over per-signal baseline

evidence: Wilson 95% lower bound 0.6566 well above Tier M floor — sample size adequate
R-multiple 0.72 below Tier S threshold (1.0); promotion is to Tier M (B/TAKE_SMALL) only, not Tier S (A/TAKE_FULL)
risks: Cohort tier may drift on next archive comparison; demotion auto-fires per §3
R-multiple distribution under D-8 review (Day-6 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7 D-8)
Portfolio regime concentration triggered (100% Choppy, threshold 0.8) — any new UP_TRI/Metal entry compounds single-regime exposure
R-multiple 0.72 reflects D-8 forced-exit dominance per project_anchor §7; true edge may compress if exit logic changes
approval would propagate overlap to mini_scanner_rules.json

proposed_rule_entry.reason: brain_step4: cohort Tier M, n=17, wr=88.2%, edge +25.3pp

decision_metadata.llm_enrichment_rationale: Step 4 tier classification is robust (n=17, Wilson lower 0.6566 above Tier M floor, +25.3pp edge over UP_TRI baseline) with promotion correctly capped at Tier M/TAKE_SMALL given R-multiple 0.72. The sector=Metal filter narrows rather than amplifies the existing UP_TRI exposure (4 of 24 open) in a Choppy-concentrated portfolio, and TAKE_SMALL sizing limits incremental concentration. Consistent with multiple prior approvals of this exact cohort (2026-04-29 through 2026-05-04) with no trader rejection on record.

proposals.title: Exposure concentration: by_signal_type×sector×regime (multi-cell) + by_regime = DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto (0.25) + DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT (0.2083); regime=Choppy at 1.0 (45.8%)

claim.what: Concentration risk: by_signal_type×sector×regime (multi-cell) + by_regime = DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto (0.25) + DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT (0.2083); regime=Choppy at 1.0; recommended action: Pause new entries in DOWN_TRI×Auto and DOWN_TRI×IT until cell shares drop below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Do not add to short side in Auto or IT regardless of signal trigger.
claim.expected_effect: Trader awareness of tail risk: If Choppy resolves into a Bull regime or sector rotation lifts Auto/IT, ~46% of the book (11 short positions clustered in two sectors) reverses simultaneously with no validated edge to absorb drawdown — correlated short squeeze on unvalidated cohort.

evidence: DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto cell share=0.25 exceeds 0.20 cell threshold (6/24 positions)
DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT cell share=0.2083 exceeds 0.20 cell threshold (5/24 positions)
Both cells map to cohort regime=Choppy__signal_type=DOWN_TRI at Candidate tier (n=3) — unvalidated edge
DOWN_TRI signal share=19/24=0.792, heavy directional short skew
Combined Auto+IT DOWN_TRI cluster = 11/24 = 45.8% of book
risks: Recommendation may be over-cautious; concentration may be edge-validated
Trader may already be aware; informational only

proposals.reversibility: Informational warning; no system mutation. Suppresses on next run if concentration drops below threshold.

decision_metadata.recommended_action: Pause new entries in DOWN_TRI×Auto and DOWN_TRI×IT until cell shares drop below 0.20 via natural Day-6 exits. Do not add to short side in Auto or IT regardless of signal trigger.
decision_metadata.tail_risk_scenario: If Choppy resolves into a Bull regime or sector rotation lifts Auto/IT, ~46% of the book (11 short positions clustered in two sectors) reverses simultaneously with no validated edge to absorb drawdown — correlated short squeeze on unvalidated cohort.
decision_metadata.llm_rationale: Portfolio is 100% Choppy regime (above 0.80 threshold) AND has two cells breaching the 20% threshold: DOWN_TRI×Choppy×Auto at 25% and DOWN_TRI×Choppy×IT at 20.83%. Both cells map only to Candidate-tier cohort (n=3), so this is unvalidated concentration — over 45% of the book is concentrated in two sector shorts with no statistical edge support. DOWN_TRI also dominates signal mix at 19/24 = 79%, just below the 85% threshold but reinforcing the directional skew.
